approach: Split the confirmed alphabet into three additive contract stores (bases/marks/attestedStacks, flat confirmedInventory derived), host the S0-S5 marks series as one new spine EditorStep between carve and mechanisms with card-based stations, and hand the mechanism gallery a typed PlacementWorklist via an optional prop. Engine logic lands as pure functions in a new engine/src/marks/ subsystem (nfc-posture-of-inventory shared table, house-target-policy-shaped output-form decision table), with the uniformity invariant enforced by a new IR-level validator check plus one new layer-c-enforce criteria row (148 to 149).

decisions.decision: Host the series as one new spine EditorStep (marks) between carve and mechanisms, stations sequenced internally with S0 as an unrendered gate
decisions.why: S1/S2 interpolate the designer's own glyphs, which the static FlowQuestion shape cannot express; the EditorStep card mechanism already exists (CharactersStep/MechanismGallery precedents)
decisions.rejected: Extending the Phase B sub-flow with more pb_* FlowQuestions, or one spine step per station

decisions.decision: Three stores + PlacementWorklist as additive optional contract fields; legacy confirmedInventory kept and derived from the stores
decisions.why: Inventory is contract-owned today and the documented convention is additive fields; deriving the flat list keeps every existing consumer working with no locked-type edit
decisions.rejected: Studio-local store split (engine needs typed input) or replacing confirmedInventory (major-bump locked-contract removal)

decisions.decision: Build nfc-posture-of-inventory as the single shared pure function and copy the house-target-policy decision-table shape for the S4 output-form proposal
decisions.why: The facet YAML already names the function as its planned derivation, and one computed table keeps the facet, proposal, unwrap stores, and blocking rules from ever disagreeing
decisions.rejected: Independent posture computation in the station and the validator (divergence is the failure mode FR-022 exists to prevent)

decisions.decision: Enforce the uniformity invariant as an IR-level check (layer-a-prime convention, tagged layer B) plus one new layer-c-enforce criteria row, bumping enforced counts 148 to 149 and band 66 to 67
decisions.why: FR-022 requires mechanical checkability against the produced keyboard; normalization form of emitted outputs is an IR property, not a lexical one
decisions.rejected: Survey-side assertion only (advisory) or a Layer A source-string regex check

decisions.decision: Ship blocked combinations as generated swallow-behavior rules (minimal A6 pull-forward) and generate stepwise backspace-unwrap stores from the attachment matrix
decisions.why: No blocking generator exists; swallow defers the Phase-C-gated A6 loudness axis without blocking FR-021, and nfd-to-nfc.ts already recognizes the unwrap rule shape
decisions.rejected: Implementing the full A6 axis now (scope creep) or pairwise NxN exclusion UI

decisions.decision: Record the nfc-to-nfd migration need as a session flag only; do not build the reverse facet-transform in this feature
decisions.why: The spec assumption and design-note open question 3 hold it as a separate decision; silently implementing would reopen a resolved v1 decline informally

plan.summary: Full plan + research + data model + contracts for the marks question series: three-store alphabet, S0-S5 card stations as one spine step, typed worklist handoff, uniformity check
plan.key_finding: Every seam the feature needs already has an in-tree precedent - the placementMap optional-prop seam into MechanismGallery, the house-target-policy decision table, the engine_resolved gate pattern, and the planned nfc-posture-of-inventory stub in the posture facet YAML - so no locked contract changes and no new architectural surface are required

tasks.summary: 37 tasks in 11 phases: Setup + Foundational (contracts/decompose/posture/store split), one phase per P1 story (US5, US1, US2, US3, US7) then P2 (US4, US6, US8), Polish (retirement, e2e, SC validation); waves 2-3 deep per phase

FR-001.title: Three distinct records: bases, marks, ordered attested combinations

FR-002.title: Whole composed character selectable as a single pick

FR-003.title: Composed pick records base+marks+stack, visibly at pick time

FR-004.title: PUA pick asks letter-or-mark; answer is a permanent declared role

FR-006.title: One prefilled attachment row per mark (attested/plausible/blocked)

FR-007.title: Unchecked base means blocked, consequence stated in row help text

FR-008.title: Single-attested-base mark shows as auto-confirmed summary, still editable

FR-009.title: Case pairs derived from alphabet case data, no separate question

FR-010.title: Marks grouped into classes; mental model confirmed per class with overrides

FR-011.title: Mental-model prefill from spread, base mechanism, spare-key affordability

FR-012.title: Input-order question only when a letter-plus-mark class exists, prefilled from import

FR-013.title: Output form is one uniform keyboard-wide decision, never mixed

FR-014.title: Any pair lacking a ready-made form: propose base-plus-mark as a notice

FR-015.title: All pairs compose + no letter-plus-mark class: propose ready-made as a notice

FR-016.title: Ambiguous case: open choice, recommended first, no Unicode/normalization wording

FR-018.title: Stacking confirmation only on evidence, else two-mark combos stay blocked

FR-019.title: Affirmative stacking shows specific attested stacks for explicit confirmation

FR-020.title: Series exit hands the gallery a complete three-group classification

FR-021.title: Blocked combinations unreachable by any ordinary key sequence

FR-022.title: Uniformity invariant mechanically checkable on the produced keyboard

FR-023.title: Alphabet edits that change decision evidence flag stations for reconfirmation

FR-024.title: No up-front marks question; every station derived from the confirmed alphabet

FR-025.title: Retire five standalone marks questions; relocate pb_mark_input_order as S3

FR-026.title: Digraphs keep their own question, with parallel wording

FR-005.title: Computed, never-displayed gate skips the series when marks list is empty

concerns.note: Baseline not fully green: 19 pre-existing studio test failures (useKeyboardArtifact.test.ts, browserPatternLibrary.test.ts, projectWorkingCopyVfs*.test.ts, articleIVProbe.test.ts) present before any 046 edit; new-failure attribution is by diff against this set

T001.did: Baseline: install/build/typecheck green; 19 pre-existing studio test failures in 4 files (useKeyboardArtifact, browserPatternLibrary, projectWorkingCopyVfs x2, articleIVProbe) documented as attributable baseline

T002.did: New contracts module confirmedAlphabet.ts: ConfirmedAlphabet/AttestedStack/DeclaredRole/AttachmentState/MarkUnit/BlockedCombination/PlacementWorklist + factories, deriveConfirmedInventory, validateConfirmedAlphabet, 10 tests

T003.did: decomposeGrapheme engine helper (NFD, order-preserving, full Mn/Mc range, null for PUA/plain/digraph); exported isCombiningMarkChar/isPrivateUseCodePoint from characterMap; 9 tests

T005.did: Additive alphabet?/marksWorklist? on SurveyPhaseResult+SurveySession; store-wise mergeAlphabets (dedupe, order-preserving stacks, last-wins roles), last-wins worklist, derived projection folded into confirmedInventory; 6 merge tests

T006.did: nfcPostureOfInventory + aggregateInventoryPosture pure functions (9 tests); facet YAML derivation flipped planned->available (facet-lint green)

T007.did: phaseBDraftStore split: picks canonical, bases/marks/attestedStacks/declaredRoles derived (orphan-free removal), lastPick contribution for US5 highlight, snapshot carries declaredRoles; chars keeps pinned verbatim setAll contract; 29 tests green incl. consumers

T008.did: Pick-commit decomposition narration in CharacterMapPane: aria-live announcement now states base->Letters, mark->Marks, combination recorded on add (grid toggle + U+XXXX escape hatch)

T009.did: AlphabetBreakdown section in BuildListView: Letters/Marks/Accented letters chips (marks on U+25CC carriers), just-added 'new' indicators, renders only when marks/stacks exist; 4 tests

T010.did: CharactersStep commits ConfirmedAlphabet (draftConfirmedAlphabet) alongside flat confirmedInventory on completion; absent for manual-flow (empty draft)

T011.did: MarksSeriesStep host: computeMarksGate S0 (never rendered), immediate completion with empty PlacementWorklist on skip, direction-aware transparency via new session lastNavigation flag, series shell + marks-continue for marked alphabets; 6 tests

T012.did: Spine step 'marks' registered between carve and mechanisms: manifest entry, expectedSpine, advance policy case, ActiveStepId unions; pinned spine oracles updated (advance.test, manifest.test, StudioShell.test)

T013.did: Gate recomputes from session.alphabet via useMemo (US1 AC2: adding a marked char makes series reachable on next advance); back-pop transparency prevents the skip gate trapping Back from mechanisms

T014.did: groupMarkClasses: function-bucket (above/below/other) + single-link Jaccard clustering over attested base sets, named ATTACHMENT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD constant; deterministic ids/labels

T015.did: proposeAttachments tri-state (attested prechecked / class-sibling plausible / blocked default) + FR-008 autoConfirmed predicate + deriveCaseCounterparts (FR-009, caseCounterpart reuse); 10 engine tests

T016.did: AttachmentStation S1 card: per-mark rows, plausible dashed-suggested chips, unchecked-means-blocked help text, auto-confirmed details summary, derived case-pair note; testid marks-attachment

T017.did: Station sequencing in the host: visibleStations list with per-station render conditions, Continue advances/completes, in-series Back; simple orthography = one screen (4 new tests)
